creator: Julie Bort, Rebecca Bellan
title: (No AI) Microsoft, Google, Amazon say Anthropic Claude remains available to non-defense customers
dc:creator: Julie Bort, Rebecca Bellan
content: Trump's Department of War feud with Anthropic won't impact other companies that are using Claude via Microsoft and Google products.
contentSnippet: Trump's Department of War feud with Anthropic won't impact other companies that are using Claude via Microsoft and Google products.

creator: Russell Brandom
title: (No AI) Anthropic’s Claude found 22 vulnerabilities in Firefox over two weeks
dc:creator: Russell Brandom
content: In a recent security partnership with Mozilla, Anthropic found 22 separate vulnerabilities in Firefox — 14 of them classified as "high-severity."
contentSnippet: In a recent security partnership with Mozilla, Anthropic found 22 separate vulnerabilities in Firefox — 14 of them classified as "high-severity."

title: (No AI) Grammarly is using our identities without permission
author: Stevie Bonifield
content:
![A screenshot of a draft Verge post in Google Docs with an AI-generated Grammarly comment using Nilay Patel’s name](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/03/grammr-crop.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Grammarly's "expert review" feature offers to give users writing advice "inspired by" subject matter experts, including recently deceased professors, as [_Wired_ reported](https://www.wired.com/story/grammarly-is-offering-expert-ai-reviews-from-your-favorite-authors-dead-or-alive/) on Wednesday. When I tried the feature out myself, I found some experts that came as a surprise for a different reason - one of them was my boss.

The AI-generated feedback included comments that appeared to be from _The Verge_'s editor-in-chief, Nilay Patel, as well as editor-at-large David Pierce and senior editors Sean Hollister and Tom Warren, none of whom gave Grammarly permission to include them in the ["expert reviews."](https://support.grammarly.com/hc/en-us/articles/38552366848781-Grammarly-Expert-Review-user-guide)

![](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/03/grammarly-ai-expert-reviews-david-pierce-comment.png?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

The feature, which [launched in August](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20250818186419/en/Grammarly-Launches-Specialized-AI-Agents-and-Writing-Surface-for-Students-and-Professionals), claims to h …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/890921/grammarly-ai-expert-reviews)
contentSnippet:
Grammarly's "expert review" feature offers to give users writing advice "inspired by" subject matter experts, including recently deceased professors, as Wired reported on Wednesday. When I tried the feature out myself, I found some experts that came as a surprise for a different reason - one of them was my boss. 
The AI-generated feedback included comments that appeared to be from The Verge's editor-in-chief, Nilay Patel, as well as editor-at-large David Pierce and senior editors Sean Hollister and Tom Warren, none of whom gave Grammarly permission to include them in the "expert reviews." 
The feature, which launched in August, claims to h …
Read the full story at The Verge.

When I tried the feature out myself, I found some experts that came as a surprise for a different reason - one of them was my boss. The AI-generated feedback included […]

title: (No AI) The AI Doc is an overwrought hype piece for doomers and accelerationists alike
author: Charles Pulliam-Moore
content:
![A bearded man wearing glasses making a face of discomfort at someone off-camera.](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/03/Screenshot-2026-03-06-at-1.32.13%E2%80%AFPM.png?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Co-director Daniel Roher. | Image: Focus Features

We are in the thick of [a massive push](https://www.theverge.com/tech/863365/national-retail-federation-show-shopping-commerce-ai) to [incorporate generative AI](https://www.theverge.com/2024/1/13/24035152/ces-generative-ai-hype-robots) into almost [every aspect of our lives](https://www.theverge.com/23610427/chatbots-chatgpt-new-bing-google-bard-conversational-ai), but it is still easy to be confused about what it is and how it works. It doesn't help that many of gen AI's proponents and detractors both speak about it with a feverish hyperbole that comes across like fantastical ad copy. And the rate at which AI firms release new iterations of their products can make it hard to keep track of what's going on in the industry as a whole.

In [_The AI Doc: Or How I Became an Apocaloptimist_](https://www.theverge.com/entertainment/880120/the-ai-doc-or-how-i-became-an-apocaloptimist-trailer), codirectors Daniel Roher and Charlie Tyrell attempt to make sense of this moment in gen AI's rise to prominence The f …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/entertainment/890806/the-ai-doc-or-how-i-became-an-apocaloptimist-review)

Co-director Daniel Roher. | Image: Focus Features

And the rate at which AI firms release new iterations of their products can make it hard to keep track of what's going on in the industry as a whole.

Read the full story at The Verge.

title: (No AI) Codex Security: now in research preview
content: Codex Security is an AI application security agent that analyzes project context to detect, validate, and patch complex vulnerabilities with higher confidence and less noise.

title: (No AI) How Descript enables multilingual video dubbing at scale
content: Descript uses OpenAI models to scale multilingual video dubbing, optimizing translations for both meaning and timing so dubbed speech sounds natural across languages.

creator: Boone Ashworth
title: (No AI) This Jammer Wants to Block Always-Listening AI Wearables. It Probably Won’t Work
dc:creator: Boone Ashworth
content: Deveillance’s Spectre I, developed by a recent Harvard grad, wants to give people control over the always-on wearables surrounding their lives. The problem? Physics.
contentSnippet: Deveillance’s Spectre I, developed by a recent Harvard grad, wants to give people control over the always-on wearables surrounding their lives. The problem? Physics.

creator: Reece Rogers
title: (No AI) Why Is Alexa+ So Bad?
dc:creator: Reece Rogers
content: I stuck Amazon’s Echo Show 15 and its Alexa+ AI assistant in my kitchen for a month. Things have not gone well.
contentSnippet: I stuck Amazon’s Echo Show 15 and its Alexa+ AI assistant in my kitchen for a month. Things have not gone well.
